---
title: "Skein Lite"
pagetitle: "Skein Lite: an agentic RAG platform"
toc: false
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import json
S = json.load(open("../evaluation/reports/site_stats.json"))
```

::: {.hero}
::: {.eyebrow}
Retrieval-augmented generation · built end to end
:::
# A shopping assistant that knows when it doesn't know {.hero-title}

::: {.lede}
Sara is the AI assistant on Aster Athletics, a demo apparel storefront. A signed-in shopper asks where their order is, wants a gift for their mum, or checks whether a legging runs small. Sara answers from store data with citations, or says she does not know, or hands off to a human. The engine underneath knows nothing about apparel: swap one folder and it becomes a different store.
:::
:::

::: {.stat-strip}
::: {.stat}
::: {.n}
`{python} S["tests"]`
:::
::: {.k}
tests, fully offline
:::
:::
::: {.stat}
::: {.n}
`{python} f"{S['routing']['tiebreak_8b_pct']}%"`
:::
::: {.k}
routing accuracy, `{python} f"{S['routing']['tiebreak_8b_escalation_recall_pct']:.0f}%"` escalation recall
:::
:::
::: {.stat}
::: {.n}
`{python} f"${S['cost']['text_turn_usd']}"`
:::
::: {.k}
per text turn
:::
:::
::: {.stat}
::: {.n}
`{python} f"{S['cost']['human_vs_ai_ratio']:.0f}×"`
:::
::: {.k}
[cheaper per turn than a human](cost.qmd)
:::
:::
:::

The hard problem in a retrieval system is refusing well. A model that always answers will confidently invent an order number, a price, or a color it never saw. Most of the work here sits in the seams around the model: a per-citation grounding check, a sampled online faithfulness judge from a different model family, and an abstain gate for thin evidence.

The other half is what runs before the model. One deterministic orchestrator routes each turn through a cheap-first cascade: `{python} f"{S['routing']['deterministic_pct']}%"` of `{python} S["routing_eval_set"]` labeled turns are decided free, and a small-model tie-break lifts accuracy to `{python} f"{S['routing']['tiebreak_8b_pct']}%"` while never missing a genuine "get me a human". Order privacy, prompt injection, and customer enumeration are refused in code, before the prompt.

Every product, review, and order is synthetic, invented for a fictional brand. What's real is the machinery: the ingestion, the hybrid retrieval, the `{python} S["golden_items"]`-item golden set that gates model changes, and the CI that blocks a regression before it ships.

## What to read next

::: {.grid-cards}
::: {.card}
### [Experience →](experience.qmd)
The shopper story: one signed-in conversation walked end to end, an order check, a gift, a sizing question, and the privacy gate that holds through all of it.
:::
::: {.card}
### [Architecture →](architecture.qmd)
The path a question takes from keypress to grounded answer: the routing cascade, the specialist lanes, hybrid retrieval, the graph and metric seams, and the one gated pipeline they all share.
:::
::: {.card}
### [Evaluation →](evaluation.qmd)
The labeled routing eval, the model-tier experiments, the offline CI gate, RAGAS, and drift monitors, with every number regenerated from a command.
:::
::: {.card}
### [Cost →](cost.qmd)
The full proof behind "`{python} f"{S['cost']['human_vs_ai_ratio']:.0f}×"` cheaper than a human": every assumption, the price of each stage of a turn, and the division.
:::
::: {.card}
### [MLOps →](mlops.qmd)
The human-gated loop: drift detection, one deduped GitHub issue, a registered candidate, shadow replay, and a person who decides. Nothing self-ships.
:::
::: {.card}
### [Decisions →](decisions.qmd)
Why Groq and Cohere, why one orchestrator instead of an agent graph, why a cheaper model swap was held on measured faithfulness.
:::
::: {.card}
### [Reliability →](reliability.qmd)
The failure modes planned for: what happens when the LLM, the vector store, or the voice service is slow or down, and the fallback for each.
:::
::: {.card}
### [Try it →](usage.qmd)
The shortest path to running it locally, signing in as the demo shopper, and talking to Sara.
:::
:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Built to be inspected.** Every claim on this site maps to code you can read, and the headline numbers load from one generated file, `evaluation/reports/site_stats.json`, so the prose can never drift from the evidence. The [Reliability](reliability.qmd) page names the known limits; naming them is what makes the rest credible. Full source on [GitHub](https://github.com/hoomanesteki/agentic-rag-knowledge-ai-platform).
:::
